item.text: I’ll inspect the repository instructions and existing files, then derive the algorithm, implement `solution.py`, and validate it with samples plus brute-force randomized checks.

The graph decomposes cleanly: every directed cycle forces all its variables equal, so each cycle contracts to one root; all remaining vertices form rooted trees. I’m implementing a tree DP over values `1..M`, with prefix sums enforcing each child value `≤` its parent value.

item.text: All three samples match. I’m now checking the actual submission against exhaustive enumeration on many small random functional graphs, including self-loops, multi-node cycles, and multiple components; then I’ll run a maximum-size performance test.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - << 'PY'
import itertools
import random
import subprocess
import sys

random.seed(387)
for case in range(250):
    n = random.randint(1, 7)
    m = random.randint(1, 4)
    a = [random.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
    expected = 0
    for x in itertools.product(range(m), repeat=n):
        if all(x[i] <= x[a[i]] for i in range(n)):
            expected += 1
    data = f\"{n} {m}\\n\" + \" \".join(str(v + 1) for v in a) + \"\\n\"
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, \"solution.py\"], input=data, text=True,
        capture_output=True, check=True
    )
    actual = int(result.stdout)
    if actual "'!= expected:
        print("MISMATCH", case, n, m, a, expected, actual)
        raise SystemExit(1)
print("250 randomized exhaustive checks passed")
PY'

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 -m py_compile solution.py
python3 - << 'PY'
import subprocess
import sys
import time

n = m = 2025
cases = {
    \"star\": [0] * n,
    \"chain\": [0] + list(range(n - 1)),
    \"binary_tree\": [0] + [(i - 1) // 2 for i in range(1, n)],
    \"one_cycle\": list(range(1, n)) + [0],
}
for name, a in cases.items():
    data = f\"{n} {m}\\n\" + \" \".join(str(v + 1) for v in a) + \"\\n\"
    started = time.perf_counter()
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, \"solution.py\"], input=data, text=True,
        capture_output=True, check=True
    )
    elapsed = time.perf_counter() - started
    print(name, f\"{elapsed:.3f}s\", result.stdout.strip())
PY
git diff -- solution.py"

item.text:
Implemented the complete solution in [solution.py](/Users/james/Library/Mobile%20Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Code/live-code-bench/LiveCodeBench/native-codex-gpt-5.6-sol-ultrafast-q1-server-tps-2026-07-13_133429/q1_abc387_f/solution.py).

Validated with:

- All provided samples
- 250 randomized exhaustive brute-force checks
- Maximum-size star, chain, binary-tree, and cycle cases
- Python syntax compilation

Complexity: `O(NM)` time and memory-efficient tree DP storage.
